text.title: langchain-rag-pipeline Core Rules
text.summary: Rules for building LangChain RAG pipelines: loaders, splitters, embeddings, retrievers, and the LCEL prompt shape.

rule.statement: Use RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter with chunk_overlap ≈ 10% of chunk_size. Zero overlap splits relevant passages across chunks.
rule.rationale: Boundary-cut sentences cause recall@k drops on queries whose answer straddles the chunk boundary.

rule.statement: Hash document bodies for embedding-cache lookup; skip re-embedding when content_hash matches. Never key by file path alone.
rule.rationale: Re-indexing the same corpus under new paths doubled embedding cost in one prod incident; content_hash dedup is mandatory.

rule.statement: In the prompt template, put retrieved context FIRST, then the question, then explicit instruction to use only the context above.
rule.rationale: Models attend to recent tokens; context-first + instruction-after produces fewer answers that ignore the retrieval.

rule.statement: Default retriever_k = 4-6. Higher k floods the prompt; add a cross-encoder reranker if you must overfetch.
rule.rationale: Accuracy plateaus near k=5 for most corpora; bigger k adds tokens without lift.

rule.statement: Bind the LCEL chain as parallel retrieval + passthrough then prompt then llm then parser. Never serialize retrieval before passthrough.
rule.rationale: RunnableParallel composition is the only shape that retrieves and forwards the question concurrently.

rule.statement: Pick the embedding model from the same provider as the LLM where possible (e.g., OpenAI text-embedding-3 with GPT-4). Cross-provider drift is a real source of retrieval misses.
rule.rationale: Same-provider embeddings tend to align better with the model's internal representations.

rule.statement: For corpora over 1M tokens, pick a hosted vectorstore (Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector) over an in-memory Chroma. In-memory dies on restart and won't share across replicas.
rule.rationale: In-memory stores cannot survive horizontal scaling; production needs durability and shared state.

rule.statement: Hold out 20% of any labeled question set as a sealed eval used only in CI; never read it during dev.
rule.rationale: Leakage of the eval set into prompts inflates scores; sealing is the only defense.
